 {"id":"bd-1z3","title":"[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.2 — Compatibility Core

Why This Exists:
Compatibility core as strategic wedge: policy-visible behavior, divergence receipts, L1/L2 lockstep, and spec-first fixture governance.

Task Objective:
Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_2/bd-1z3_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-1z3/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-1z3/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.2] Implement deterministic compatibility fixture runner and result canonicalizer." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:44.163128134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T09:40:35.689847016Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T09:40:35.689820326Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-2","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z3","depends_on_id":"bd-2kf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:20.220218653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1z5a","title":"[10.17 bug] Replace verifier-economy and replay-capsule trust surrogates with canonical signed capsule verifier","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs` still treats attestation signatures as basically "algorithm label + matching key + non-empty value", `verify_capsule_integrity` still only checks hash presence, and `crates/franken-node/src/connector/verifier_sdk.rs` still accepts any non-empty `signature` field. That means the verifier economy, replay capsules, and public trust scoreboard can present as independently verifiable while still admitting decorative signatures and integrity placeholders.

What this bead must do:
Replace these trust surrogates with one canonical signed-capsule verification kernel shared across verifier-economy and connector surfaces, per the ownership map. The implementation should be stronger than a basic "verify blob signature" path: canonical serialization, detached signature verification, chunked or Merkleized trace commitments for streaming replay, minimal fraud-proof witnesses, and strict binding between claim text, capsule contents, verifier identity, and replay verdict. Verified evidence should be cheap to re-check, impossible to spoof by shape, and rich enough to support public scoreboards without hand-waving.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Attestation signatures and replay capsule integrity in `verifier_economy/mod.rs` and `connector/verifier_sdk.rs` use real canonical serialization and signature/hash verification; presence-only or shape-only acceptance is removed.
2. Capsule integrity binds at least: input state, execution trace commitment, output state, expected-result commitment, verifier identity, claim/version metadata, and issuance timestamp / freshness envelope.
3. Trace commitments are chunked or Merkleized so external verifiers can validate subtraces and emit minimal fraud proofs without replaying everything from scratch.
4. Verifier reputation / public scoreboard updates consume only verified attestations and verified capsules; unverifiable evidence is rejected or explicitly quarantined, never silently scored.
5. The connector verifier SDK and verifier-economy surface share one canonical verification kernel instead of duplicating divergent local checks.
6. Adversarial fixtures cover forged same-length signatures, payload swaps under reused signatures, truncated capsules, cross-claim replay, mismatched trace commitments, and stale/freshness-window violations.
7. Add a checker that fails if signature-present / hash-present shortcuts or equivalent trust surrogates return to replacement-critical verifier paths.
8. Deliver unit tests, integration/E2E capsule replay fixtures, structured logs, benchmark evidence, and machine-readable artifacts proving external replay and trust-score updates are grounded in real verification.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 3. Unit tests cover canonical serialization, chunked or Merkleized subtrace verification, freshness-window handling, payload swaps, truncated capsules, mismatched commitments, and regression checks against decorative signatures.

6. Structured logs expose stable CAPSULE_VERIFY_* and VERIFIER_SCORE_* event families with required fields: trace_id, capsule_id, verifier_id, claim_id, commitment_digest, decision, reason_code, and fraud_proof_id.
7.  {"id":"bd-1z5a.11","title":"[bd-1z5a] Add signed replay-capsule verification to verifier economy","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs` currently recomputes replay capsule integrity hashes, freshness, and trace commitments, but the `ReplayCapsule` artifact itself still carries no detached verifier signature or signer key binding. That means a capsule can be internally self-consistent yet still be forgeable by any actor who can populate matching fields.

What this task must do:
- add signed replay-capsule payload binding for the verifier-economy capsule artifact,
- verify that signature during registration/access,
- keep the implementation shared with connector verifier helpers rather than inventing a one-off local signature path,
- add focused inline regression tests for tampered signature / tampered payload / wrong signer cases,
- route cargo-heavy validation through rch only if attempted.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Replay capsules in the verifier economy carry verifier key/signature material and are rejected if the signature does not validate over the canonical capsule payload.
2. Tampering with bound capsule fields after signing is rejected even if the integrity hash is recomputed.
3. The signature helper logic lives in shared verifier code, not duplicated ad hoc in verifier_economy.
4. Focused tests cover success, tampered payload, and wrong signer key.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkPond","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:32:21.012877953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T09:49:34.603358056Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T09:49:34.603323602Z","close_reason":"Closeout audit 2026-03-11: acceptance criteria satisfied by current tree. crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs now binds ReplayCapsule signatures to canonical replay_capsule_signature_payload(...) and verifies through cached Ed25519 key checks, with focused regression tests covering tampered signatures, forged claim hash binding, and wrong verifier key.  {"id":"bd-nbwo.2","title":"[bd-nbwo support] Bind workspace replay capsule signature to full manifest","description":"Why this exists:
`sdk/verifier/src/capsule.rs::compute_signing_payload` currently signs only `capsule_id`, `schema_version`, `expected_output_hash`, `payload`, and `inputs`. That means an attacker can tamper `claim_type`, `input_refs`, `created_at`, `creator_identity`, or `metadata` under a reused structural signature, and `verify_signature` will still accept the capsule.

What this bug must do:
- bind the workspace replay capsule structural signature to the full manifest surface, not just a subset
- keep the structural-only posture explicit while making reused-signature tampering fail closed
- add focused regressions for manifest-field tampering under a reused signature

Acceptance criteria:
1. `compute_signing_payload` changes when any manifest field changes, including `claim_type`, `input_refs`, `created_at`, `creator_identity`, and `metadata`.
2. `verify_signature` rejects reused-signature capsules whose manifest fields were tampered after signing.
3. Inline tests cover at least `claim_type`, `creator_identity`, and `metadata` tampering, plus a stable happy path.
4. Cargo-heavy validation uses `rch` only.","notes":"Implemented full-manifest structural signature binding in `sdk/verifier/src/capsule.rs`.

What changed:
- added a shared length-prefixed helper for deterministic signing payload construction
- extended `compute_signing_payload` to bind manifest `description`, `claim_type`, `input_refs`, `created_at`, `creator_identity`, and `metadata` in addition to the previously-bound fields
- added focused regressions proving reused signatures fail after tampering `claim_type`, `input_refs`, `created_at`, `creator_identity`, and `metadata`

Validation:
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rosefox_bdnbwo2_tests cargo test -p frankenengine-verifier-sdk capsule -- --nocapture` passed: `33 passed; 0 failed`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rosefox_bdnbwo2_check cargo check -p frankenengine-verifier-sdk --all-targets` passed
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rosefox_bdnbwo2_clippy cargo clippy -p frankenengine-verifier-sdk --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed
- `rustfmt --check sdk/verifier/src/capsule.rs` passed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseFox","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:43:05.656339794Z","created_by":"RoseFox","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:47:24.363028638Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T04:47:24.363003040Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nbwo.2","depends_on_id":"bd-nbwo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:43:05.656339794Z","created_by":"RoseFox","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-nbwo.3","title":"[bd-nbwo support] Bind workspace replay capsules to declared input refs","description":"Why this exists:
`sdk/verifier/src/capsule.rs` exposes `CapsuleManifest.input_refs` in the public workspace replay-capsule schema, but `replay()` currently trusts the actual `inputs` map without enforcing that the declared input set matches it. A capsule can therefore present one input inventory in its signed manifest while replaying another, which weakens the public capsule contract even in the structural-only SDK surface.

What this bug must do:
- fail closed when `manifest.input_refs` and `inputs` keys diverge
- reject duplicate declared refs as ambiguous manifest state
- add focused regressions while preserving the happy path

Acceptance criteria:
